limitations: Operator chat timestamps inside this slice are approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
This log preserves the preset-selection recovery and redeploy truth even though the client does not export exact per-message telemetry.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator noticed that a saved preset starting with 0 behaved differently and suspected that an older preset overlap was still steering behavior even after the main runtime regressions had been addressed.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Cleared the player preset root at Custom\\PluginData\\FrameAngel\\Player\\presets so stale saved presets could no longer overlap with the current alpha retest.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Confirmed the chooser seam in code: the preset UI already had a none token internally, but it only surfaced that choice when there were zero saved presets, and otherwise catalog refresh silently selected the alphabetically first real preset. That made a stale 0-prefixed preset a plausible hidden default winner.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.13, refreshed the foundation outputs and package report, redeployed the modern_tv raw dev alpha proof, and also refreshed the baseline direct-player raw asset from the same validated player core.

decisions: 0.6.13 is the next semantic version because the preset chooser logic changed after 0.6.12 had already been built and committed.
The live authority seam for this slice remains dev_deploy raw Custom artifacts, not the .var package.
Preset selection must stay on an explicit none state until the operator intentionally chooses a preset.
openQuestions: Does 0.6.13 alpha fully eliminate the hidden default-preset overlap when the preset root is clean?
Should the preset browser listing mismatch be addressed next, or kept separate from the core player/runtime lane?
notes: The BrowserAssist preset listing mismatch remains logged but out of scope for this slice.
The baseline player and the modern_tv proof both ride the same 0.6.13 player core in this dev_deploy alpha.
